score.method: Heuristic repository-readiness score. It is not a course grade and does not prove runtime correctness.

strengths: Broad learning progression across 8 detected week modules.
Multi-language portfolio spanning Python, browser development, TypeScript, SQL, and Node-oriented work.
Strong documentation habit with 133 documentation files.
Substantial hands-on practice with 54 detected mini-project/daily-challenge roots.
Animated README assets create a distinctive and memorable portfolio identity.
Rich Git history (285 commits) demonstrates sustained iteration.
Several stronger projects already separate domain logic from user interaction, which is a good base for testing.

findings.title: Directory names collide by case or punctuation
findings.detail: Week1Python/Day3Dictionaries/Exercises/ExercisesXP, Week1Python/Day3Dictionaries/Exercises/ExercisesXP+
findings.recommendation: Merge content into one canonical directory using git mv on a dedicated branch.

findings.title: Directory names collide by case or punctuation
findings.detail: Week6DatabasesAndNodejs/Day1IntroductionToDatabases/Exercises/ExercisesXP, Week6DatabasesAndNodejs/Day1IntroductionToDatabases/Exercises/ExercisesXP+
findings.recommendation: Merge content into one canonical directory using git mv on a dedicated branch.

findings.title: Package-manager lockfiles are ignored
findings.detail: package-lock.json, pnpm-lock.yaml, yarn.lock
findings.recommendation: Remove lockfile patterns from .gitignore and commit the selected lockfile for reproducible installs.

findings.title: Very low exercise-level automated test coverage
findings.detail: 1 of 124 detected exercise roots contain test files (0.8%).
findings.recommendation: Start with pure functions and domain classes in Tic-Tac-Toe, Hangman, OOP modules, TypeScript validators, and Node utilities.

findings.title: No GitHub Actions workflow
findings.detail: No tracked YAML workflow was found under .github/workflows/.
findings.recommendation: Add a read-only quality workflow for Python syntax, formatting, linting, tests, and report generation.

findings.title: No root package-manager lockfile
findings.detail: package.json exists, but no recognized lockfile is tracked.
findings.recommendation: Choose one package manager, run its install command, and commit exactly one lockfile.

findings.title: Node test command has no discoverable test files
findings.detail: The root test script uses node --test, but no JavaScript/TypeScript test filename was detected.
findings.recommendation: Add .test.js/.spec.js tests for the strongest browser and Node modules.

findings.title: Source archives stored at repository root
findings.detail: Week1Python (2).zip (206.0 KB), Week1Python.zip (205.7 KB)
findings.recommendation: Confirm they are redundant, keep a local backup, then remove them from Git or move them to release assets.

findings.title: README snapshot is stale
findings.detail: README revision fd10e5e does not match current HEAD 0dad94c5e93c.
findings.recommendation: Generate repository statistics during the audit instead of hard-coding them.

findings.title: Exact duplicate file content
findings.detail: 2 files share the same SHA-256: .eslintignore, .prettierignore
findings.recommendation: Keep the canonical copy and replace intentional duplicates with references where practical.

findings.title: Exact duplicate file content
findings.detail: 3 files share the same SHA-256: Week5MiniProjectAndTypeScript/Day4AdvancedTypeScriptConceptsAndApplications/DailyChallenge/.gitignore, Week5MiniProjectAndTypeScript/Day4AdvancedTypeScriptConceptsAndApplications/Exercises/ExercisesXP/.gitignore, Week5MiniProjectAndTypeScript/Day4AdvancedTypeScriptConceptsAndApplications/Exercises/ExercisesXPGold/.gitignore
findings.recommendation: Keep the canonical copy and replace intentional duplicates with references where practical.

findings.title: ESLint ignore path does not match repository casing
findings.detail: Configured: Week5MiniprojectAndTypescript/**/compiled/; actual Week5 roots: Week5MiniProjectAndTypeScript.
findings.recommendation: Use exact canonical paths after merging the duplicate Week5 directories.

findings.title: Exercises without local README documentation
findings.detail: 31 of 124 detected exercise/project roots lack a local README.
findings.recommendation: Add a short README with goal, concepts, run command, expected behavior, edge cases, and improvement ideas.

findings.title: Non-portable or inconsistent directory names
findings.detail: Detected 7 directory path(s). Examples: Week1Python/Day3Dictionaries/Exercises/ExercisesXP+ (plus sign); Week2OOP/Day5MiniProject/DailyChallenge/OOPQuizz (likely misspelling); Week3JavaScriptandDOM/Remote LearningJSAndDOM (space); Week4AdvAsynchronousJavaScript/Day5Fetch&AsyncAwait (ampersand); Week5MiniProjectAndTypeScript/Day1Miniproject/DailyChallange (likely misspelling); Week6DatabasesAndNodejs/Day1IntroductionToDatabases/Exercises/ExercisesXP+ (plus sign); Week6DatabasesAndNodejs/Day3DatabaseConcepts2/Exercises/ExercicesXPGold (likely misspelling)
findings.recommendation: Adopt one convention: Week/Day folders in PascalCase; project folders in PascalCase; Python files in snake_case; web files in kebab-case or camelCase.

findings.title: Placeholder npm scripts
findings.detail: Scripts without real behavior: dev, build
findings.recommendation: Replace each placeholder with a working command or remove it until the integrated application exists.

findings.title: README text names files that do not exist
findings.detail: Detected 104 unresolved backticked file reference(s). Examples: README.md → .github/workflows/quality.yml; Week1Python/Day2ListsIteratingAndFormattingData/Exercises/ExercisesXP/README.md → Week1Python/Day2ListsIteratingAndFormattingData/Exercises/ExercisesXP/2.0; Week1Python/Day2ListsIteratingAndFormattingData/Exercises/ExercisesXP/README.md → Week1Python/Day2ListsIteratingAndFormattingData/Exercises/ExercisesXP/2.0; Week1Python/Day2ListsIteratingAndFormattingData/Exercises/ExercisesXPNinja/README.md → Week1Python/Day2ListsIteratingAndFormattingData/Exercises/ExercisesXPNinja/collections.Counter; Week1Python/Day3Dictionaries/Exercises/ExercisesXP+/README.md → Week1Python/Day3Dictionaries/Exercises/ExercisesXP+/datetime.strptime; Week1Python/Day3Dictionaries/Exercises/ExercisesXP+/README.md → Week1Python/Day3Dictionaries/Exercises/ExercisesXP+/collections.Counter; Week1Python/Day3Dictionaries/Exercises/ExercisesXPGold/README.md → Week1Python/Day3Dictionaries/Exercises/ExercisesXPGold/datetime.strptime; Week1Python/Day3Dictionaries/Exercises/ExercisesXPGold/README.md → Week1Python/Day3Dictionaries/Exercises/ExercisesXPGold/dict.get; Week1Python/Day5MiniProject/Exercises/Hangman/README.md → Week1Python/Day5MiniProject/Exercises/Hangman/game.py; Week1Python/Day5MiniProject/Exercises/Hangman/README.md → Week1Python/Day5MiniProject/Exercises/Hangman/words.py
findings.recommendation: Regenerate directory trees from git ls-files and remove unsupported quality claims.

findings.title: SVG files are marked binary
findings.detail: Text-based SVG diffs will be hidden from normal Git reviews.
findings.recommendation: Prefer '*.svg text eol=lf' unless the SVGs are generated and intentionally opaque.

rename_plan.reason: Two Day3 HTTP/Form directories use inconsistent capitalization.
rename_plan.notes: Compare files and merge before removing the old path.

rename_plan.reason: Two top-level Week5 directories differ only by capitalization and content placement.
rename_plan.notes: Merge child-by-child on a dedicated branch; do not overwrite the existing destination.

rename_plan.reason: Normalize a misspelling, space, or shell-sensitive character.
rename_plan.notes: Review assignment links and README references before approval.

rename_plan.reason: Spaces make shell commands and links less predictable.
rename_plan.notes: Update Markdown and HTML links after moving.

rename_plan.reason: Ampersands require escaping in several shells.
rename_plan.notes: Use git mv and update references.

rename_plan.reason: Normalize a misspelling, space, or shell-sensitive character.
rename_plan.notes: Review assignment links and README references before approval.

rename_plan.reason: Duplicate-looking source archive at repository root.
rename_plan.notes: Compare archive contents before deletion; archive locally first.

rename_plan.reason: Root source archives duplicate material already tracked as files.
rename_plan.notes: Prefer keeping the ZIP outside Git history after confirming it is unnecessary.

next_actions.title: Fix syntax errors before every other refactor
next_actions.why: A syntax error blocks trustworthy testing and CI.
steps: Run the studio again until Python syntax is 100% valid.

next_actions.title: Merge duplicate Week5 and Week4 paths on a branch
next_actions.why: Case-colliding directories are fragile across Windows, Linux, GitHub, and tooling.
steps: Create a dedicated cleanup branch in GitHub Desktop.
Move unique files into the canonical destination with git mv.
Update links and run the audit.
next_actions.definition_of_done: Only one canonical path exists for each module and every local link resolves.

next_actions.title: Select npm and commit package-lock.json
next_actions.why: Dependencies cannot be reproduced reliably without a tracked lockfile.
steps: Run npm install once at repository root.
next_actions.definition_of_done: npm ci works from a fresh clone.

next_actions.title: Remove duplicate Week1 ZIPs from the repository root
next_actions.why: Archives inflate clones and duplicate source history.
steps: Back them up outside the repository.
Delete them from Git or attach them to a release if preservation is necessary.
next_actions.definition_of_done: No redundant source ZIP remains in the tracked root.

next_actions.title: Regenerate README facts from the repository
next_actions.why: A polished README loses trust when statistics, paths, and roadmap status contradict the code.
steps: Use the generated audit data rather than hard-coded counts.
Keep roadmap phases aligned with actual folders.
next_actions.definition_of_done: README paths resolve and its snapshot matches current HEAD.

next_actions.title: Add a minimal read-only GitHub Actions quality workflow
next_actions.why: CI prevents syntax, link, formatting, and test regressions before merge.
steps: Run Python compile checks and npm quality scripts.
Fix failures before adding stricter gates.
next_actions.definition_of_done: A push and pull request both produce a green quality workflow.

next_actions.title: Test five portfolio anchors first
next_actions.why: Testing every historical exercise at once is inefficient; high-value modules produce faster confidence.
steps: Test one Node filesystem or data utility.
next_actions.definition_of_done: At least five representative projects have deterministic tests and CI runs them.

next_actions.title: Build the integrated Nova Learning Dashboard capstone
next_actions.why: The repository becomes much stronger when historical exercises feed one deployable full-stack product.
steps: Design a small API and database schema.
next_actions.definition_of_done: A public deployment demonstrates authentication, persistence, API integration, tests, and responsive UI.
